Ezekiel is to compare the kingdom of Judah to a lioness. He must compare the kings of Judah to a lion’s whelps; they were cruel and oppressive to their own subjects. The righteousness of God is acknowledged when those who have terrified and enslaved others are themselves terrified and enslaved. When people who profess religion form relationships with ungodly persons, their children usually grow up following the principles and customs of a wicked world. Advancement to authority reveals the ambition and selfishness of men's hearts; and those who spend their lives in mischief generally end them by violence.

Jerusalem was a vine, flourishing and fruitful. This vine is now destroyed, though not uprooted. She has by wickedness made herself like tinder to the sparks of God’s wrath, so that her own branches serve as fuel to burn her. Blessed be God, one Branch of the vine referred to here is not only become a strong rod for the scepter of those who rule, but is Himself the true and living Vine. This will be for rejoicing to all the chosen people of God throughout all generations.
